evals.prompt: I modified three hooks last week and want to verify I didn't introduce regressions before cutting the next release. Run the benchmark suite and compare against the last baseline.
evals.expected_output: User runs bash scripts/benchmark/test-hooks.sh and gets exit code 0 if all hooks pass functional tests. Runs aggregate-results.sh which outputs new score, compares hook_score against previous baseline in audit-artifacts/benchmark-baseline.json. If regressed, reports which hook(s) failed.

evals.prompt: We're about to add 5 new skills to ValidationForge. What's the minimum bar they need to meet so the overall benchmark grade doesn't drop from A to B?
evals.expected_output: User checks current aggregate score from prior baseline. Calculates: skills weight 20%, current skill_score N%. New skills must have avg frontmatter/description score ≥ X to keep aggregate ≥ 90. (Actual math depends on current baseline.)
